what_it_does: Defines a single ATTACHMENT_TYPE dict declaring an 'images' attachment type: an image bound to a channel addressed as image://<id>, target_kind='address', multi=True; desc explains the manifest projects channel → [images] so a channel becomes the addressed/hierarchical home for its visual artifacts.
observations: Inline comment notes the address is opaque and resolved by stripping the scheme then calling cc_images.get_image.
desc lists the operations enabled: store, generate-into, read, comment, cross-reference.
Frames the channel as the hierarchical home for its images via a manifest projection channel → [images].
standouts: Documents the resolution path (strip scheme → cc_images.get_image) for the opaque image address.
Enumerates the verbs (store/generate-into/read/comment/cross-reference) the channel-as-image-home supports.
conventions: Same single-dict descriptor pattern as siblings.
Inline comment goes beyond labeling target_kind to specify the actual resolver call.
notes: Registry entry for 'images': opaque image://<id> attachments resolved via cc_images.get_image, making the channel the hierarchical home for its visual artifacts.
questions: What is cc_images.get_image and how does the manifest projection channel → [images] get built?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares the images attachment type and its channel-as-home semantics per the docstring.
intention_signals: Aims to make a channel the addressed, hierarchical home for images so visual artifacts can be stored, generated, read, commented on, and cross-referenced within it.
novelty: Treating a channel as the hierarchical place where images live, with an opaque image:// address and a named resolver.
contribution: Contributes the declarative definition of the images attachment type, its image:// address scheme, resolver path, and the channel-as-image-home projection.
summary_for_embedding: A descriptor module declaring the 'images' channel attachment type: opaque image://<id> addresses resolved via cc_images.get_image, multi=True, making the channel the home for its images.
intention_for_embedding: To register images as channel-bound addressed attachments so a channel becomes the hierarchical home for its visual artifacts.
